name: Speed of light in vacuum
description: The speed of electromagnetic radiation in a vacuum. Defines the metre since 1983.

name: Planck constant
description: Relates a photon's energy to its frequency: E = hν. Defines the kilogram since 2019.

name: Reduced Planck constant
description: h / (2π). Used extensively in quantum mechanics: ħ = h/2π.

name: Elementary charge
description: The electric charge of a single proton (or the magnitude of an electron's charge). Defines the ampere since 2019.

name: Boltzmann constant
description: Relates average kinetic energy of particles to temperature. Defines the kelvin since 2019.

name: Avogadro constant
description: Number of particles in one mole of a substance. Defines the mole since 2019.

name: Universal gas constant
description: R = N_A · k_B. Appears in ideal gas law: PV = nRT.

name: Gravitational constant
description: The proportionality constant in Newton's law of gravitation. One of the least precisely known fundamental constants.

name: Standard gravity
description: Standard acceleration of gravity at Earth's surface. An exact defined value by ISO 80000-3.

name: Electron mass
description: Rest mass of an electron.

name: Proton mass
description: Rest mass of a proton.

name: Neutron mass
description: Rest mass of a neutron.

name: Atomic mass unit
description: 1/12 of the mass of a carbon-12 atom. Used for atomic and molecular masses.

name: Vacuum permittivity
description: Electric constant / permittivity of free space: ε₀ = 1/(μ₀c²).

name: Vacuum permeability
description: Magnetic constant / permeability of free space.

name: Fine-structure constant
description: α ≈ 1/137. Characterises the strength of the electromagnetic interaction between particles.

name: Faraday constant
description: F = N_A · e. Electric charge per mole of electrons. Fundamental to electrochemistry.

name: Stefan–Boltzmann constant
description: Relates blackbody radiated power to temperature to the fourth power.

name: Pi
description: Ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter. Transcendental and irrational.

name: Euler's number
description: Base of the natural logarithm. Appears in compound interest, probability, and complex analysis.

name: Golden ratio
description: φ = (1 + √5)/2. Appears in geometry, art, architecture, and nature (phyllotaxis).

name: Rydberg constant
description: Relates to atomic spectra (hydrogen energy levels): En = −R∞hc/n².

name: Bohr radius
description: The most probable distance between nucleus and electron in a ground-state hydrogen atom.
